to: make the child’s welfare the paramount consideration

conditions.text: a court determines any question with respect to— (a) the upbringing of a child; or (b) the administration of a child’s property or the application of any income arising from it

to: have regard to the general principle that any delay in determining the question is likely to prejudice the welfare of the child

conditions.text: in any proceedings in which any question with respect to the upbringing of a child arises

to: presume that involvement of that parent in the life of the child concerned will further the child's welfare
have regard in particular to the ascertainable wishes and feelings of the child concerned

conditions.text: in the circumstances mentioned in subsection (4)

to: not make the order or any of the orders

conditions.text: the court is considering whether or not to make one or more orders under this Act with respect to a child
it considers that doing so would be better for the child than making no order at all

to: have regard to the physical, emotional and educational needs of the child

conditions.text: in the circumstances mentioned in subsection (4)
